Tests uploaded by Shadow Autotest2 from shadow-39vm5 for review-ldiskfs received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.7/x86_64: (https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sessions/20c6471a-98a6-11e5-8fa3-5254006e85c2). Ran 6 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Looks good. 

On a related topic, does test_32 test lfsck and lfs migrate of inodes?  That is something that should be added in a new patch if not.

Patch Set 2:

Hi Andreas,

For lfsck, t32_test() contains the following tests:

        if [ "$ff_convert" != "no" -a $(facet_fstype ost1) == "ldiskfs" ]; then
                $r $LCTL lfsck_start -M $fsname-OST0000 || {
                        error_noexit "Start OI scrub on OST0"
                        return 1
                }

                # The oi_scrub should be on ost1, but for test_32(),
                # all on the SINGLEMDS.
                wait_update_facet $SINGLEMDS "$LCTL get_param -n \
                        osd-ldiskfs.$fsname-OST0000.oi_scrub |
                        awk '/^status/ { print \\\$2 }'" "completed" 30 || {
                        error_noexit "Failed to get the expected 'completed'"
                        return 1
                }

                local UPDATED=$($r $LCTL get_param -n \
                                osd-ldiskfs.$fsname-OST0000.oi_scrub |
                                awk '/^updated/ { print $2 }')
                [ $UPDATED -ge 1 ] || {
                        error_noexit "Only $UPDATED objects have been converted"
                        return 1
                }
        fi


For lfs migrate, t32_test() contains the following tests:

                # migrate files/dirs to remote MDT, then move them back
                if [ $(lustre_version_code mds1) -ge $(version_code 2.7.50) -a \
                     $dne_upgrade != "no" ]; then
                        $r $LCTL set_param -n   \
                                mdt.${fsname}*.enable_remote_dir=1 2>/dev/null

                        echo "test migration"
                        pushd $tmp/mnt/lustre
                        for dir in $(find ! -name .lustre ! -name . -type d); do
                                mdt_index=$($LFS getdirstripe -i $dir)
                                stripe_cnt=$($LFS getdirstripe -c $dir)
                                if [ $mdt_index = 0 -a $stripe_cnt -le 1 ]; then
                                        $LFS mv -M 1 $dir || {
                                        popd
                                        error_noexit "migrate MDT1 failed"
                                        return 1
                                }
                                fi
                        done

                        for dir in $(find ! -name . ! -name .lustre -type d); do
                                mdt_index=$($LFS getdirstripe -i $dir)
                                stripe_cnt=$($LFS getdirstripe -c $dir)
                                if [ $mdt_index = 1 -a $stripe_cnt -le 1 ]; then
                                        $LFS mv -M 0 $dir || {
                                        popd
                                        error_noexit "migrate MDT0 failed"
                                        return 1
                                }
                                fi
                        done
                        popd
                fi

Patch Set 2:

CMD: shadow-44vm3 tar xjvf /usr/lib64/lustre/tests/disk2_4-zfs.tar.bz2 -S -C /tmp/t32
 ......
 dd: writing `/tmp/t32/mnt/lustre/tmp_file': No space left on device
 1+0 records in
 0+0 records out
 0 bytes (0 B) copied, 0.332897 s, 0.0 kB/s
  conf-sanity test_32b: @@@@@@ FAIL: dd failed

It's strange that disk2_7-*.tar.bz2 were not tested. Performing the tests manually.

Patch Set 2:

Rather than testing these images manually, it makes more sense to investigate why they are not being tested automatically.  Otherwise, they will not be tested automatically in the future and much of the value will be lost.

Maybe /tmp is a ramdisk and this is too small to hold all of the images?  Would it be better to use /var/tmp, or possibly uncompress the images into OSTDEV1, OSTDEV2, MDSDEV1, etc. so that we are sure there is enough space to hold them? 

As for testing LFSCK, it makes sense to test it for ZFS these days also.  I suspect LFSCK testing was restricted to ldiskfs during development, before ZFS support was added in 2.5.58.

Patch Set 2:

Thank you Andreas for the suggestions. It turns out that I forgot to add disk2_7-*.tar.bz2 into lustre/tests/Makefile.am. I'm updating the patch.

parents.subject: LU-5710 all: fourth set of corrected typos and grammar errors

commit.subject: LU-7192 tests: conf-sanity test 32c supports DNE on ZFS
commit.message:
LU-7192 tests: conf-sanity test 32c supports DNE on ZFS

1. ZFS needs import pool before tunefs.
2. previous 2.4 ZFS disk image contains 100M file, which will cause
   'migrate' used up all OST space and fail, recreate the image.
3. generate test images for 2.7-formatted ldiskfs and zfs with
   DNE striped directories feature enabled and update t32_test()
   to test that.

Test-Parameters: alwaysuploadlogs envdefinitions=SLOW=yes,ENABLE_QUOTA=yes,ONLY=32 mdtfilesystemtype=zfs mdsfilesystemtype=zfs ostfilesystemtype=zfs mdtcount=4 testlist=conf-sanity
Change-Id: Ib35e4cb80d6beee9695b26844c4e6a99e88adb89
Signed-off-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>

Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/16663
